title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.title: How to Make Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled — Easy Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled. This easy Indian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled
easy Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled recipe
authentic Indian Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled
lowCompetitionKeywords: Indian Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled Recipe
h2Headings: What is Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Indian Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled — a beloved Indian classic

description: Experience the smoky perfection of Tandoori Chicken, marinated in a vibrant blend of yogurt and aromatic spices, then expertly grilled over charcoal. This classic Indian dish promises tender, juicy chicken with a charred exterior, bursting with authentic flavors that will transport your taste buds straight to the heart of India. It is a beloved centerpiece for any Indian dinner.

ingredients.notes: or any cooking oil, for basting

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make deep slits on the chicken pieces, especially on the breast and thigh areas, to allow the marinade to penetrate well.

instructions.title: First Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, 1 teaspoon of Kashmiri red chili powder, and half a teaspoon of salt. Rub this mixture all over the chicken pieces, ensuring it gets into the slits. Let it rest for 15 minutes.

instructions.title: Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, remaining lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, black pepper powder, and salt to taste. If using, add a pinch of red food color. Mix well to form a smooth, thick marinade.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces to the second marinade, ensuring each piece is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor and tenderness. The longer the marination, the better the taste.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30-40 minutes before cooking, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal to create a medium-hot cooking zone. The coals should be glowing red with a thin layer of ash. Ensure the grill rack is clean and lightly oiled.

instructions.title: Grill the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly on the hot grill rack. Cook for about 25-35 minutes, turning every 5-7 minutes to ensure even cooking and charring. Baste the chicken frequently with a mixture of melted butter and mustard oil during the last 10-15 minutes of grilling to keep it moist and add flavor. Cook until the chicken is tender, cooked through, and has a beautiful charred exterior.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill. Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the top and garnish with fresh coriander leaves and sliced onion rings. Serve immediately with mint chutney, naan bread, or a fresh salad.

tips: For the most authentic flavor, use full-fat plain yogurt. Greek yogurt can also work but thin it slightly with water to achieve the right consistency.
Making deep slits on the chicken pieces is crucial for the marinade to penetrate deeply, ensuring the chicken is flavorful and moist throughout.
While grilling, if the chicken starts to burn too quickly, move it to a slightly cooler part of the grill or raise the grill rack to control the heat. Basting frequently prevents drying out.
article: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is a beloved Indian Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
